channel.title: The Arena Partners - Exit Planning Blog
channel.description: Exit planning strategies for trade business owners ($5M-$50M). Build transferable companies. Achieve premium exits.

item.title: The 3 Numbers That Determine What Your Service Business Is Actually Worth
item.description: Most service business owners think they know what their company is worth. But buyers care about three numbers: EBITDA, the multiple, and owner dependency score. The gap between a 3x and 7x multiple on a $2M EBITDA business is $8 million.
item.dc:creator: Jean Louis Hardy

item.title: How to Value an Electrical Business for Sale
item.description: Exit planning insights for electrical contractors - how to value an electrical business for sale

item.title: Business Exit Planning for Contractors: The Stories That Tell You Why It Matters
item.description: Business exit planning for contractors starts years before the exit. See what Pete Schopen built over 17 years, what Toys R Us failed to do, and why the consolidators are coming for your industry.
item.dc:creator: Jean Louis Hardy

item.title: Business Exit Strategy for Service Companies: Culture, Systems, and the $12B Lesson
item.description: Business exit strategy for service companies starts with what Southwest Airlines, Zappos, and Mailchimp knew: culture and systems together create exits that honor everything you built.
item.dc:creator: Jean Louis Hardy

item.title: How Long Does It Take to Sell a Business? Orkin, Schopen, and Mailchimp Answer
item.description: How long does it take to sell a business? The transaction is 6-12 months. The preparation is the work. Orkin built for 63 years. Schopen for 17. Mailchimp for 20. What each timeline produced - and what yours should look like.
item.dc:creator: Jean Louis Hardy

item.title: How to Increase Business Valuation Before Selling: Three Stories That Show the Way
item.description: How to increase business valuation before selling. Domino's went from $3 stock to $500 with radical honesty and technology. Starbucks turned a commodity into a $100B experience. DeJoria built premium positioning from nothing. The lessons for trade business owners.
item.dc:creator: Jean Louis Hardy

item.title: How to Sell a Plumbing Business: What Rollups Are Really Buying
item.description: Learn how to sell a plumbing business the right way. Real stories from Wrench Group, Schopen Pest, and Orkin show what buyers actually pay for - and why systems matter more than revenue.
item.dc:creator: Jean Louis Hardy

item.title: Owner-Dependent Business Valuation: The Hidden Discount in Your Exit Price
item.description: Owner-dependent business valuation is the most common hidden discount in trade business exits. The E-Myth, Toyota, and Kodak explain what it costs - and how to fix it.
item.dc:creator: Jean Louis Hardy

item.title: Selling a Business at 60: Colonel Sanders, Mary Kay, and Dave Thomas Disagree
item.description: Selling a business at 60 or starting a new chapter at 60 - the evidence is overwhelming that age is not the constraint. Colonel Sanders started at 65 with $105/month. Mary Kay started at 45. Dave Thomas got his GED at 61. Here is what they knew.
item.dc:creator: Jean Louis Hardy

item.title: Succession Planning for Trade Businesses: Three Stories That Tell You Everything
item.description: Succession planning for trade businesses determines whether your legacy survives. Sears lost 132 years in a decade. Ford's genius became his company's greatest threat. Orkin built systems that outlived the founder by 60+ years. Here's what each story teaches.
item.dc:creator: Jean Louis Hardy

item.title: What Buyers Look for When Buying a Business: The Wrench Group Checklist
item.description: What buyers look for when buying a business in the trades. Wrench Group evaluated 3,000+ companies per year. GE shows what financial engineering looks like to institutional buyers. Netflix's Keeper Test reveals what retention-based thinking produces.
item.dc:creator: Jean Louis Hardy

item.title: What Is My HVAC Business Worth? The Multiple Gap Explained
item.description: What is my HVAC business worth? The answer depends on whether you own a business or a job. Wrench Group, McDonald's, and the E-Myth reveal the difference - and the 3x vs 14x multiple gap.
item.dc:creator: Jean Louis Hardy
